title: lavallea ceylanica
intro: Lavallea ceylanica, also known as Ceylon cinnamon or false cinnamon, is a plant species that belongs to the family Lauraceae and is native to Sri Lanka. The medicinal properties of Lavallea ceylanica include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its various health benefits. The health benefits of Lavallea ceylanica include aiding digestion, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Lavallea ceylanica can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
